$comment: D-FFI-TCL-DESCRIPTOR (SQLite testfixture arc, C13 2026-07-18): RUN witness for the TCL REFCOUNT macros + the Tcl_DString named layout added to tcl.json (dissolves D-FFI-TCL-REFCOUNT-MACROS; the dominant post-C9 S0001 test-TU blocker -- test_bestindex/test_func/test_mutex use Tcl_IncrRefCount, test_tclvar Tcl_DecrRefCount, test8 Tcl_DStringValue). Tcl_Incr/DecrRefCount are field-poking macros in real tcl.h that libtcl does NOT export; libtcl DOES export Tcl_DbIncrRefCount/Tcl_DbDecrRefCount (nm -D) doing the same ++/--refCount (+free), so tcl.json models the macros as function-macros over those EXPORTED funcs (the ckalloc/ckfree precedent) -- no Tcl_Obj named-field layout, no ~30-signature ripple, and eager-import stays safe (the C9 lesson). A registered C command pins a Tcl_Obj via Tcl_IncrRefCount, reads 40 while pinned, releases via Tcl_DecrRefCount, then reads a Tcl_DString's `string` field via Tcl_DStringValue(&ds) -> ((&ds)->string) [needing Tcl_DString's REAL named layout] after appending element "2" -> 40+2=42; libtcl calls it back on the eval. elf-x86_64 only (tcl.json availableObjectFormats:[elf]); the CI ubuntu x86_64 legs run it (libtcl8.6 installed), native-arm64 arch-gates the run, windows/macos compile-only. RED-ON-DISABLE: remove Tcl_DbIncrRefCount/Tcl_DbDecrRefCount, the Incr/Decr/DStringValue macros, or the Tcl_DString `string` field from tcl.json -> S0001 / field-resolve failure -> fails to compile (or ld.so exit 127 if only the Db* symbol is dropped).
